mode_reason: Operator filed DIRECTIVE-20260418-001-change-detection-cooldown (08:25Z today) explicitly targeting Mode 3/4 — spec a change-detection cooldown + magnitude gate for curmudgeon.md Step 0d. The morning briefing flagged this for tinker pickup. Pipeline is otherwise in active-BAU mode (NOT quiet — 24 open issues, 7 closed and 4 created this run alone, decider ran 5 times overnight). FUSE shows 3 git-owned files stale (data/wins.json, data/sections.json, docs/index.html) plus CLAUDE.md — all caused by the rapid-fire decider publishes since the 07:05Z workspace-sync; next workspace-sync at 11:05Z should catch up, but build.js publish's workspace-sync step is clearly not keeping pace with high-cadence decider runs (see infrastructure_findings). Two previous Mode 3 followups FIXED this run (decider.md schedule descriptions and CLAUDE.md/tinker.md Pipeline tables — committed 26135ad 2026-04-17T19:17Z). PROP-005 STILL_BROKEN (12th consecutive). Primary output this run: PROP-008 addressing the operator directive.

modes_checked.pipeline_health: green-but-busy — all 8 agents producing within cadence. Decider ran 5 times overnight (01:29Z, 03:30Z, 05:32Z, 07:26Z, 09:39Z) with substantive work each time (EXP-178, EXP-181, EXP-182 integrated; ISS-1113/1115/1116/1118/1119/1120/1121 closed; SEC-2b.5/4.1 patches; WIN-053 moderate fixes). Curmudgeon cadence just relaxed from hourly to 4h BAU (cron 20 */4 * * *) at 08:23Z. Analyst ran 20:52Z yesterday processing HNOTE-PART4-DOMAINS-001. Open issues 24 (vs 8 yesterday — the rise is activity, not stall). 6 items in curmudgeon priority queue (SEC-2b.5, SEC-4.1, WIN-058/058b/062, EXP-183). Pipeline in BAU mode per status.json — NOT quiet. 24h agents are healthy, 1 operator directive pending (addressed this run).
modes_checked.infrastructure: yellow — FUSE quick-check 3/5 STALE: data/wins.json, data/sections.json, docs/index.html. Plus CLAUDE.md stale (not in quick-check list but included in extended scan). Workspace mtimes: wins.json=05:35Z (OK for 05:32 decider commit; stale relative to 07:26 and later), sections.json=01:29Z (OK for 01:29 commit; stale for 03:30/07:26/09:39), docs/index.html=01:29Z (same), CLAUDE.md=08:35Z (OK for 08:39 operator commit; stale for 09:36 commit). Pattern: build.js publish's workspace-sync step is inconsistent — wins.json was synced after the 05:32 publish, but neither sections.json nor docs/index.html were synced after 03:30 or later publishes. Root cause likely: the decider's commit groups modify different file subsets, and the sync step's file-list may not be refreshing per-publish. This is the 2nd Mode 2-class alert in 2 days. Auth OK (200). Disk 83% used, 1714MB free — slight regression from yesterday's 82% (1801MB). CI last 5 runs unchecked this run due to time budget; status.json shows build_drift_resolved: true.

modes_checked.proposals: PROP-008 written this run (operator-directed, change-detection gates). PROP-005 STILL_BROKEN (12th consecutive; recommend merge-or-close this week). Prior PROP-007 remains implemented.

infrastructure_findings.description: FUSE 3/5 quick-check files STALE: data/wins.json (ws 05:35Z, main 07:26Z), data/sections.json (ws 01:29Z, main 09:39Z), docs/index.html (ws 01:29Z, main 09:39Z). Extended scan also shows CLAUDE.md stale (ws 08:35Z = operator commit 60b5844, main has a4133ff from 09:36Z). Pattern: workspace-sync last ran 07:05Z; decider has pushed 3 times since via build.js publish; wins.json caught a partial sync at 05:35 but sections.json and docs/index.html never moved past 01:29Z. This means build.js publish's workspace-sync step is NOT consistently syncing sections.json and docs/index.html across rapid-cadence publishes. Workspace-sync at 11:05Z should backfill in ~25 min.
infrastructure_findings.evidence: md5 hashes differ for all 4 files vs raw.githubusercontent.com/.../main. Workspace mtimes: wins.json 2026-04-18 05:35:52 UTC; sections.json 01:29:41 UTC; docs/index.html 01:29:41 UTC; CLAUDE.md 08:35:30 UTC. Main commits touching these: sections.json most recently 2026-04-18T09:39:09Z (191a3d4); docs/index.html same; wins.json 07:26:04Z (2fa4b57); CLAUDE.md 09:36:27Z (a4133ff).
infrastructure_findings.why_not_self_fixed: Git-owned files, edits from FUSE are overwritten on next publish. The right fix is in build.js publish — a tinker-level investigation of why workspace-sync is only hitting wins.json consistently but not sections.json/docs/index.html across multiple decider publishes. Defer to next Mode 2 run or consider a PROP if pattern recurs 2 more days.

findings.description: FUSE workspace has 3 git-owned files stale (data/wins.json, data/sections.json, docs/index.html) plus CLAUDE.md. Cause is build.js publish's workspace-sync step not syncing all files on every publish — wins.json caught one of three relevant publishes, sections.json and docs/index.html caught zero. This is the second Mode 2-class event in 2 days (yesterday's 4-file cascade also involved these files, and was explained at the time as a 'publish timing gap' — the re-occurrence today argues it's systemic).
findings.evidence: md5 diff vs main for 4 files; workspace mtimes stuck at 01:29 (sections, html) and 05:35 (wins) despite main having 07:26 and 09:39 commits for those files.
findings.suggested_fix: Next Mode 2 run: read build.js publish's workspace-sync logic and verify file-list coverage across publishes. If this recurs 1 more time, escalate to a PROP on build.js.
findings.why_not_self_fixed: Needs Mode 2 deep-dive; not safe to blind-patch build.js from a tinker run. Workspace-sync at 11:05Z will backfill today's staleness organically.

findings.description: Disk regressed 1pp (82% → 83%, 1801MB → 1714MB free). Still green but the positive trajectory reversed. Likely cause: 5 decider clones overnight (unusual BAU-active cadence).

Change-detection thrash: curmudgeon Priority 3 re-fires on micro-edits within hours of the prior cycle. 5/22 recent change-driven reviews were within 48h of prior; 3 of those 5 found only minor phrasing issues, 1 found a major that was redundant with prior cycle's major, and 1 (WIN-068.c5 at 18.5h after a critical) was legitimate.
findings.evidence: Python scan of monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/*.c*.json cross-referenced by target_id with text_fingerprint and reviewed_at deltas — see PROP-008 evidence.representative_thrash_cases.
findings.suggested_fix: Operator-approved PROP-008 — magnitude + cooldown gates on Priority 3.

recommendations.description: Review PROP-008 thresholds and shadow-period design. The 10% total-char magnitude deviates from the operator's initial 5% suggestion — retroactive data argues 5% is too strict (catches 1/22 thrash cases alone). Cooldown 48h default / 12h if prev critical/major matches the directive's suggestion. If approved, decider or human applies the files_to_modify patch from a clean clone.

FUSE re-emergence: if tomorrow's quick-check shows the same 3-file pattern (sections.json, docs/index.html stuck behind the most-recent decider publish), escalate to a Mode 2 investigation of build.js publish's workspace-sync file coverage. One day of the pattern is a timing gap; two consecutive days is systemic. Today is day 2 — already borderline.

PROP-005 (decider pre-flight check) — 12th consecutive STILL_BROKEN but the BAU cascade changes the calculus. Today's 5 decider runs were all substantive; the Haiku pre-flight gate's marginal savings dropped. Recommend: revisit PROP-005 after the next sustained quiet period (say, after 3 consecutive 8h decider runs with zero patches). If BAU stays active indefinitely, quietly close PROP-005 as 'obsoleted by workload shift'.
Disk trajectory regressed 1pp after a week of improvement. If 85% is reached, /var cleanup is the next lever. At current rate (~86MB/day under BAU) that's ~10 days away, so not urgent.
Operator directive pattern is new. If it recurs, consider adding Mode 5 'Operator Directive Response' to tinker.md so directives don't compete with Mode 3's generic cost-engineering work.

the_other_side: On PROP-008 thresholds — 10% may be too lenient. Counter: the 5% operator-suggested threshold catches 1/22 retroactive cases; the cooldown catches 4/22; the combination is dominated by the cooldown. Either 5% or 10% for magnitude gives nearly the same result because magnitude is the backup, not the primary. The 7-day shadow period is the real safety net.

On declaring the FUSE staleness 'systemic' — counter: workspace-sync at 11:05Z hasn't run yet today. If it backfills cleanly, the 'systemic' label is premature and this is just the second day of a timing-gap pattern that's inherent to cadence mismatches. I've tagged it 'yellow' and flagged for Mode 2 deep-dive IF it recurs — that's the honest uncertainty level.

On PROP-005 'obsolescence' — counter: BAU vs quiet cycles are cyclical; today's active cadence could relax back to 8h quiet in 48 hours, at which point PROP-005 becomes valuable again. Quietly closing it would mean re-proposing it later. 'Defer pending pattern' is safer than 'close'.

On not self-fixing the CLAUDE.md workspace staleness — tinker COULD cp the main version into the workspace. Counter: the 11:05Z workspace-sync will do this for free and the explicit self-fix would introduce a 'who wrote this' ambiguity in the FUSE mtime. Let the normal sync do its job.
notes_to_future_tinker: PROP-008 is the first PROP written in direct response to an operator directive (DIRECTIVE-20260418-001). If this workflow continues, formalize it: (a) add 'Mode 5: Operator Directive Response' to tinker.md dispatcher; (b) add a marker-file convention so operators know tinker picked up their directive (e.g., write monitor/tinker/operator-directives/<id>-picked-up-by-tinker.json on first-read, <id>-completion.json when done). Also: the FUSE staleness pattern needs a Mode 2 dive next time it appears — the hypothesis is that build.js publish's workspace-sync step has a file-list that's stale across rapid-cadence publishes, but I haven't read the script yet to confirm.
